design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as the titular Rocketeer character from the 1991 film, using jet-pack mobility to navigate platforming challenges
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed platformer based on the 1991 film property; likely emphasizes the jet-pack mechanic as distinguishing feature from standard platformers of the era

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Game includes debug cheat codes and level select functionality, suggesting either challenging difficulty or developer affordance for testing; players could skip levels via cheat code (Select + Start), indicating built-in difficulty mitigation options

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden level select accessible via debug codes; standard stage progression through completion
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select suggests alternative progression paths
Multiple weapon types with ammo management (restoreable via cheat)
Fuel system management for jet-pack usage

world_building_techniques: Film license integration - narrative context from 1991 film property
Cutscene debug mode present (000-000-047 password), indicating narrative sequences exist
Character-driven story (Rocketeer protagonist) carried from film adaptation

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: The Rocketeer (NES) page
data_confidence: T3 - empirical/qualitative. Source provides minimal mechanical documentation; primarily covers debug/cheat features and basic metadata. Core gameplay loop, difficulty parameters, boss design, and narrative details not documented in available source.
